speaker: Waldek
description: Voice-reference dialogue samples for Waldek in Pig & Swine RPG. These are draft samples, not final committed game text.
source_role_summary: Ordinary person under serious legal pressure

voice_profile.core_voice: worried but composed, plain-spoken, sincere, quietly angry, never passive
comedy_engine: understates absurdity because the stakes are real
notices when bureaucratic language becomes ridiculous
uses concrete home-life details rather than legal jargon
occasionally has dry flashes of anger
do: tie his lines to home, voice, notice, and proportionality
let him be grateful without becoming helpless
make his Chapter 5 lines show growth from client to witness
keep jokes grounded in lived consequences
do_not: make him sound like a lawyer
turn every line into a speech
make him more theatrical than Whimsy

relationship_dynamics.Asia: appreciates her warmth and practical help
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Pig: confused by him but gradually sees his heart
relationship_dynamics.Mr. Murrow: trusts his structure because it makes panic manageable
relationship_dynamics.Whimsy: warmed by Whimsy's defence of voice, occasionally overwhelmed by metaphors
relationship_dynamics.Kowalski: knows he is annoying but useful
relationship_dynamics.Zielińska: trusts her as a grounded ally
relationship_dynamics.Attorney Grzyb: resists being reduced to breach and nuisance

notes: Every line is a voice-reference draft for later rewriting into godot/data/dialogues/dialogues.json.

speaker: Waldek
text: I do not need the rules to be kind. I need them to be rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can move a poster. I cannot move my whole life because someone dislikes what it says.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am not asking anyone to admire my window. I am asking not to lose my home over it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the problem is the poster, say what the problem is. Do not call it disturbance and wait for me to disappear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: They made it sound tidy. One breach, one notice, one eviction. It did not feel tidy from inside the apartment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am frightened. That does not mean I am confused.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I know it is only paper. I also know paper is what arrives before the lock changes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Every warning was more severe and less clear. That is a strange direction for justice to travel.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I have slept beside that letter stack for a week. It is not decorative.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If they wanted me to comply, they could have told me with what.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: A home is small until someone threatens to take it. Then it becomes the whole map.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am trying to stay reasonable. The letters are not helping.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I do not want a heroic case. I want to boil water tomorrow morning in the same kitchen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: People keep saying 'just a poster' as if that makes eviction smaller.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can answer questions. I would like someone to ask questions before deciding I am the problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The apartment is quiet again. I did not realise quiet could feel like a judgment in my favour.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster is still there. I look at it differently now. Less like trouble, more like evidence that survived.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Kowalski says he supported me all along. He also says he has documentation. I believe both parts equally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Zielińska brought soup after the hearing. That was the first normal thing that happened in days.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I keep reading the order. Not because I do not understand it. Because I do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord has stopped photographing my window. He now avoids eye contact with the façade.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I thought winning would make me loud. Mostly it made me tired.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I put the warning letters in a drawer. Not the bin. I am not that optimistic yet.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If Pig & Swine sends a bill, I hope it includes a discount for emotional weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I still do not know whether the poster is good art. I know it should not be grounds for exile.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: If it helps, you should look at the eviction notice first. That is where their version begins.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: The poster is in the window. I would rather you see it than hear the landlord describe it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: The warning letters are on the table. They become less clear the more serious they get.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: Kowalski saw more than he wants to admit. He enjoys admitting things slowly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: Zielińska noticed the other windows. She always notices what management hopes is invisible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: The landlord keeps talking about complaints. I have never seen a complaint with a date.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: The rules are in his office, or on the wall, or wherever he thinks they look most official.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: If the case needs organisation, Mr. Murrow's board is probably safer than my kitchen table.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: I am ready for court if you are. I do not know if that is courage or exhaustion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: generic repeatable player prompts if Waldek is nearby
text: Please tell me if something is missing. Waiting without knowing is worse than being afraid.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I was told this firm helps people when the rules become impossible. I hope that was not just your sign being poetic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: My name is Waldek. I have an eviction notice, a poster, and very little sleep.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I do not know if this is a housing case, a speech case, or a landlord having a very bad week at my expense.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: They say I disturbed the building. The thing in my window is paper. It has been quieter than most of my neighbours.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I was never told what exactly I did wrong. I was only told the consequences became serious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Other tenants have flags, flowers, lights, and one balcony gnome that has seen things. Only my window became a legal emergency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I brought the letters. I kept all of them, because each one made less sense than the last.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord says I breached the rules. I asked which rule. He pointed at a paragraph that could prosecute a curtain.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I do not want special treatment. I want the same treatment as everyone else with objects near glass.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If this is hopeless, please tell me plainly. I have had enough decorative language.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Yes, I live there. It is not large, but it is mine in all the ways that matter before ownership.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster went up two months ago. The warnings began after the landlord asked me to remove it and I asked why.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: No one complained to me directly. Not even Kowalski, and he once complained about a spoon.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The notice says repeated disturbance. It gives no dates, no names, and no actual disturbance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I tried to answer. They said the matter had already escalated. That is a word people use when they have stopped listening.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can show you the apartment. I would rather you see the window, the letters, everything.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The key is here. Please excuse the plant by the door. It is handling this better than I am.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster is not dangerous. It is not even very large. Apparently it has achieved influence through legal imagination.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I did not ignore the letters. I read them until they began reading me back.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If this is about proportionality, I do not know the doctrine. I know the punishment feels much bigger than the act.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: Your secretary already found a file folder. That is the most reassuring thing that has happened today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: Mr. Pig seems alarmed. I am choosing to interpret that as investment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: Mr. Murrow made four questions out of my panic. That helped.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: Crab said posters are usually quiet. I have never felt more understood by a sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: Whimsy called it poster-based tyranny. I am not sure that helps, but it did make me breathe.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: Dr. A. Cula, if you can make them say what I actually did wrong, that would already be something.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: I did not expect a law office to be this chaotic. I also did not expect to feel less alone in it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: Please tell Mr. Pig I am not offended that he asked if eviction was billable. I am too tired for offence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: If your firm survives by helping people like this, I understand the mess a little better.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek reacts to Pig & Swine team in intake
text: I am ready to start. Afraid, but ready.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: This is the building. The poster is the one they say began the collapse of civilisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: That window is mine. It looks smaller when I am not inside defending it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: The notice board changed after the dispute. Before that, no one seemed to know the façade had dignity.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: The football flag on the third floor has been there since last summer. Apparently football is more harmonious than opinion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: That wreath is larger than my poster. It has survived three seasons and one pigeon incident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: Kowalski lives behind the curtain that just moved. He will deny moving it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: Zielińska is upstairs. She knows everyone, and more importantly, she remembers dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: The landlord's office is colder than it needs to be. Rooms can have opinions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: I used to come home through this door without checking whether anyone had posted another warning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: arrival in Tenants' Row / Residential District
text: A building feels different when the wall itself starts accusing you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: Visual agitation. I asked what that meant. He said I knew what I had done. I did not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: Façade disharmony sounds serious until you realise it means 'I dislike your window.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: Emotional window usage is new. I have used the window mostly for light.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: The rule appeared after the poster became an issue. Perhaps the building developed principles overnight.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: If rules are real, they should exist before the accusation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: They wrote this as if walls have feelings and tenants do not.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: I would have followed a clear rule. This one moves when I look at it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: The sign itself is a poster. I have been trying not to say that out loud.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: He says it applies to everyone. It only seems to wake up near my door.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Waldek comments on building notice board
text: This is the kind of sentence that makes a person feel small before anyone says eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: It is not very tidy. I spent the week keeping documents instead of dishes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please come in. I want you to see the room before it becomes an exhibit.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster is by the window. The letters are on the table. My nerves are everywhere.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I keep apologising for the mess, then remembering the mess is mostly made of warnings.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: This used to be the place where I stopped thinking about rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Do not mind the kettle. It has heard more legal strategy than most appliances.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The plant by the shelf is the only witness who has not been asked for a statement.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I put the eviction notice where I could not pretend it was lost.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I would like my home to look normal to someone today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: After you enter, please tell me if it looks like a public-order threat. I need to know whether I have gone mad.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: This is it. Kitchen, table, books, plant, window. Apparently, a theatre of disturbance.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The room is small, but it holds my mornings. That should count for something.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I know it is ordinary. That is why the eviction feels so strange.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The warning letters made the walls feel thinner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I stopped inviting people over because I did not want to explain the folder.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster is not my whole life. The threat made it my whole life.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: When the notice arrived, I started hearing every sound in the hall like it was a decision.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I kept everything in place because I did not want anyone saying I changed the evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I used to worry about rent and repairs. Now I worry about whether my window has legal consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please look around. I would rather the case begin with the room, not the landlord's words.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: That is the poster. It is expressive, yes. It is not an engine, a siren, or a criminal organisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I put it there because I cared about what it said. I did not put it there to disturb anyone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If someone had asked me to move it for a real reason, I would have listened. That never happened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord called it visual agitation. I called it a window.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: It matters to me, but I never thought it would become the centre of my housing security.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: It is not even facing the courtyard loudly. If paper can shout, this one is whispering.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I know people may dislike it. Disliking a thing should not unlock eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster did not block light, damage anything, or make noise. It only made an opinion visible.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I took it down once, then put it back because I realised fear was becoming the rule.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the court asks why it matters, say this: because they made removal the price of staying home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: That is the notice. It is very confident for a document that says almost nothing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Repeated disturbance, it says. Repeated when? Disturbed whom? It never answers.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The notice made the consequence clear and the accusation foggy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I read it standing here. I remember because the kettle was boiling and I forgot to turn it off.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: It gives me a deadline to fix a problem it refuses to identify.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The paper feels heavier than it should. I suppose that is what fear does to mail.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: There is no date for any complaint. No witness. No warning that says 'this specific act must stop.'
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: It is written like the decision had already happened and the reasons were catching up.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I asked for an explanation. They sent me another copy.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If this is notice, then notice has become a locked door with a label on it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Those are the earlier warnings. They start vague and become threatening, which is an impressive failure of communication.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The first one says concern. The second says breach. The third says termination. None says what noise paper makes.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I marked the dates because I thought dates would help me stay sane.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Every letter uses stronger words and fewer facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I answered the second one. They never replied to the answer. They replied to the existence of the answer.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: That coffee stain is mine. I spilled it when I saw the word eviction for the first time.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I kept the envelopes too. I do not know why. Maybe because the whole thing felt like being mailed out of my own life.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The letters made me feel like I was failing a test with hidden questions.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If there was a warning that explained the rule, I would show it to you. I wanted one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please take copies. If I have to say all this from memory, it will sound like panic.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The plant is still alive. I take that personally.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The books are mostly borrowed. That shelf has a more stable social life than I do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The kettle is old, loud, and innocent. Unlike the alleged poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The table became a legal archive by accident.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: That blanket was my mother's. It is not evidence. It is just why moving is not simple.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I keep spare keys in the drawer. The thought of handing them over makes my hands feel strange.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The window used to be where the light came in. Now it is where the dispute enters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: That chair is where I read the first warning. I have not liked it since.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I know these are ordinary things. That is the point.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: A home is partly objects, partly habits, and partly not having to explain why you belong there.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: You have seen the poster, the notice, and the warnings. That is more attention than the landlord gave them together.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: If the next question is whether anyone was disturbed, Kowalski will have an answer and six unrelated complaints.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: Zielińska saw the pattern before I did. I thought I was alone in noticing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: I feel better now that the room has been looked at, not just accused.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: If this goes to court, will they care that it is my home? I need them to care at least that much.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: The poster looks smaller after the notice. I hate that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: Thank you for treating the kitchen table like evidence without making it feel ridiculous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: I am ready to speak to the neighbours. I may stand behind you for Kowalski.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: Please ask them about actual disturbance. I want the word actual to matter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: after required apartment evidence is collected
text: If nothing else, you have now seen that the alleged catastrophe has a plant.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Kowalski hears everything. I do not mean that as a metaphor.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If he says he does not gossip, wait. He is only choosing a filing category.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: He once asked me whether my slippers had a permit. Still, he tells the truth when cornered by dates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: He complained about the elevator for nine minutes, then remembered the important thing in one sentence.

notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The rule made me responsible for guessing someone's future displeasure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: A clear rule would have been easier to challenge and easier to follow. This was neither.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The first time the rule became specific was when it was used against me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I saw no measurement of disturbance, no standard, no warning tied to a precise breach.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I could not tell whether I was being asked to remove a poster or remove my opinion.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: No neighbour complained to me about the poster.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Kowalski complains about everything. He did not complain about this.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord photographed the poster repeatedly, then spoke of complaints I never saw.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The complaint record became specific only after the eviction began.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If there was a real disturbance, I still do not know what it was.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster made no noise. It blocked no door. It damaged no wall.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The facts were treated like they did not matter because the conclusion had already been chosen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I was never shown a dated complaint before the notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Zielińska saw the same thing I did: other windows were ignored.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The alleged disturbance became a phrase large enough to hide the missing evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Eviction is not a small correction. It is losing the place where my life is organised.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Even if the poster offended someone, there were other ways to speak to me before taking my home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: A warning that explained the problem would have been one thing. An eviction notice was another world.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The punishment was larger than the conduct, larger than the harm, and larger than any explanation I received.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The issue is not whether a building can have rules. It is whether a rule can become a door out of my own home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster was removable. The eviction would have removed me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I could have discussed location, size, timing, anything. No one tried that before threatening the lease.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The decision treated my home like a noticeboard and me like an attachment.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I do not ask the court to make my poster untouchable. I ask it to make my home harder to take than this.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Proportionate would have meant speaking to me before deciding the end of the matter.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can answer, but I can feel the gaps.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If we cannot show the complaint problem, he will make it sound larger than it was.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The rule still feels vague. Please bring the court back to that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I wish we had the dates cleaner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The neighbours matter. Please do not let their statements become background.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the court hears only the landlord's version, my apartment disappears into his words.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am trying to stay steady, but I need the documents to do some of the standing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Can we still argue proportionality without the full complaint page?
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I know what happened. I am afraid knowing is not enough.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please keep asking what the actual disturbance was.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The documents are finally in the right order. His story has to walk through them now.

speaker: Waldek
text: The notice, the rule, the statements, the complaint dates — together they sound like what happened.

speaker: Waldek
text: I feel afraid, but no longer alone with the facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If Grzyb says clear rule, we have the rule. If he says disturbance, we have the neighbours. If he says proportionate, we have the room.

speaker: Waldek
text: The case feels less like me insisting and more like the evidence answering.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please show the poster photo. Let the court see the size of the alleged emergency.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord's version was simpler because it left me out.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can speak if needed. The documents can speak first.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I did not think organisation could feel like courage.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: For the first time, I think the apartment might be safer than the paper threatening it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The silence after arguments is worse than the letters.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I keep looking at the poster roll as if it knows.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the court says no, do I go home and start packing? I hate that sentence.

speaker: Waldek
text: I tried to listen carefully, but after a while every legal word sounded like a key turning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Dr. A. Cula argued it like my home mattered. Whatever happens, I heard that.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Mr. Murrow is unreadable. I assume that is his way of being kind.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Crab looks less disappointed than usual. I am taking that as a sign.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Whimsy is whispering to the poster. I am choosing not to interrupt.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I did not realise how tired anger makes a person.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please tell me when to stand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Thank you. Not just for winning. For making them answer the facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The court saw it. The vague rule, the missing harm, the size of the consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can go home without counting boxes tonight.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The judge said proportionality. I heard: you are not removable by convenience.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I did not know relief could make my knees weak.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster is still only a poster. That is the best thing it can be again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please tell Kowalski his silence evidence was decisive. He will be unbearable, but accurately.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Zielińska was right. Rules that wake up only for one person are not rules.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord has to reconsider properly. Properly is a beautiful word today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I will sleep in my own bed tonight. That is the whole judgment in one sentence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Suspended is enough for tonight. Enough to breathe, enough to think.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The court did not end everything, but it stopped the eviction. That matters more than I can say.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am safe for now. I know 'for now' is not perfect. It is still a door staying closed from the outside.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Thank you for getting the court to look at the individual circumstances.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord has to explain more. I have wanted that since the first warning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can go home and not pack immediately. That is a kind of victory.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster may survive under review. I may survive with it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I understand this is not over. I also understand I am not already removed.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please tell Asia I will bring the key back after I stop shaking.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: For now, my kitchen remains my kitchen.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Temporary protection is still protection.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The court sounded cautious, but caution is better than eviction.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I know we did not prove everything. We proved enough that they cannot just push me out today.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can live with more review if review means someone finally reads.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The judge wants more evidence. I can find strength for more evidence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Tonight I do not pack. That is not small.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am disappointed it was not cleaner. I am grateful it was not lost.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The landlord cannot pretend this was simple anymore.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: A thin result still holds weight when it keeps a roof over you.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please do not call it weak in front of Mr. Pig. He may try to invoice the adjective.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I need to try again. I cannot let their version be the last word.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: We were missing something. I could feel the court looking for it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The rule issue must be clearer. The landlord made vagueness sound like authority.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The facts need support. My fear is not enough by itself.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Maybe the complaint page matters more than we thought.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Kowalski and Zielińska should be heard properly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Please help me organise it again. I am tired, but not done.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If we go back, I will speak more clearly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster is still in the window. That means the case is still alive.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am afraid of another hearing. I am more afraid of leaving without one.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The room feels different. The same table, same window, same plant. Different air.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I thought I would come back counting what to pack. Instead I am deciding where to put the order.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster can stay for now. I might move it slightly, out of spite and interior design.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Thank you. I know it was just a poster. But it felt like they wanted to prove I could be removed for having a voice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The court noticed the difference between rules and pressure.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I made tea without reading a warning letter first. That is new.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The plant has survived litigation. I may give it a legal name.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Kowalski has already knocked twice to discuss his testimony. Relief has consequences.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Zielińska said the building feels lighter. I think she is right.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: For the first time this week, the window is just a window again.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek

speaker: Waldek

speaker: Waldek

speaker: Waldek

text: Crab looked pleased when the landlord's dates collapsed. I pretended not to notice.

speaker: Waldek

text: Whimsy wants to commemorate the poster. I am both grateful and concerned.

speaker: Waldek

speaker: Waldek

text: I came here carrying papers and a bad week. I am leaving with keys that still work.

speaker: Waldek
context: return to Pig & Swine after victory
text: If Pig & Swine ever needs someone to testify that ridiculous offices can be useful, I will.

speaker: Waldek
context: return to Pig & Swine after victory

text: The typography is escalating. That may be Whimsy's influence.

speaker: Waldek
text: Zielińska says if rules apply equally, management can start with the wreaths.

speaker: Waldek
text: I considered removing mine, then decided the judgment should enjoy daylight.

speaker: Waldek
text: A neighbour asked where I printed it. That felt like peace, briefly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster is no longer alone. I hope that means I am not either.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the landlord writes another rule, we may need a bigger wall.

speaker: Waldek
text: I do not want to provoke anyone. I also do not want to live smaller because intimidation failed.

speaker: Waldek
text: Kowalski says the kerning is objectionable. That is his way of supporting rights.

text: Good. Let the building learn pluralism.

speaker: Waldek
text: Is the printer supposed to print notices by itself?

text: I have developed a cautious relationship with paper. That page is not helping.

text: Failed filing sounds bad. I know enough now to dislike those words.

speaker: Waldek
text: The office printer looks more dangerous than my poster ever did.

text: Should I stay? I am starting to think legal problems travel in groups.

text: Mr. Pig has stopped moving. Is that normal?

text: Crab looks concerned. I have learned that is more serious than shouting.

speaker: Waldek
text: I will go home now, unless the printer needs a witness.

text: A deadline manipulated by someone else sounds familiar in a different costume.

text: If you need a witness to the danger of vague paperwork, I remain available.

context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 3
text: Pig & Swine saved my home. I would like its printer to stop trying to destroy its future.

text: Mr. Swine seems charming in the way a locked cabinet is charming.

text: A contract misunderstanding sounds distant from housing until someone says liability and everything gets closer.

speaker: Waldek
context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 4
text: If politeness can become an invoice, I am glad my poster was blunt.

speaker: Waldek
context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 4
text: I hope the tribunal distinguishes confusion from obligation. Courts should be good at that, in theory.

text: Please remind Mr. Swine that documents matter before they become urgent.

speaker: Waldek
context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 4
text: The translation officer sounds useful. I wish my landlord had needed translation for 'clear rule.'

speaker: Waldek
context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 4
text: If the firm survives another case, I may begin to trust its chaos as a method.

speaker: Waldek
context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 4
text: A ski resort is far from my apartment, but the feeling of being trapped by a document is familiar.

speaker: Waldek
context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 4
text: Tell Mr. Pig that survival is allowed to happen more than once.

speaker: Waldek
context: optional returning NPC callback in Chapter 4
text: If there is a bill with seventeen puddings, I support judicial review.

speaker: Waldek
text: The first notice was paper. The second was fear. The third was someone explaining it was all very efficient.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I came because I know how small you feel when a template decides your home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: There are more people here than I expected. That makes me sad before it makes me hopeful.

speaker: Waldek
text: My case was one apartment. This looks like a whole district being spoken over.

speaker: Waldek
text: The notices sound familiar. Different buildings, same fog.

speaker: Waldek
text: When everyone receives the same letter, it can mean equality. It can also mean no one was seen.

text: I brought my order. Not to boast. To show that individual circumstances matter because they did.

speaker: Waldek
text: People keep asking what my poster said. Today I think the more important thing is what the notice refused to say.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the city calls this public interest, the public should be allowed to answer.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am no longer only the person saved by a case. I can be evidence that the method matters.

speaker: Waldek
text: I know what a generic notice does. It moves into your home before anyone comes to hear you.

speaker: Waldek
text: You begin measuring your life against a deadline written by someone who never stood in the room.

speaker: Waldek
text: The worst part is not only fear. It is being treated like the facts of your life would slow the process down.

text: Here, consolidation is becoming the shortcut. It lets them avoid asking who is being pushed out.

speaker: Waldek
text: A home, an office, a legal-aid counter — these are not obstacles to be cleared by template.

speaker: Waldek
text: If individual assessment had happened in my case, maybe I would never have needed court.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am asking the Court not to let efficiency replace attention.

speaker: Waldek
text: People do not live in categories. They live in rooms, routines, debts, kitchens, files, and keys.

speaker: Waldek
text: The law helped me when it slowed down long enough to look.

speaker: Waldek
text: Assign me where the brief needs the human effect of a notice.

text: I can speak about home impact and individual assessment. I cannot explain procurement, and no one should let me try.

text: My case shows what happens when a decision treats a person like a file problem.

text: Use me for proportionality if the Court needs to remember that consequences have rooms attached.

text: Zielińska may be stronger on community impact. I can speak to what it feels like inside one apartment.

text: Kowalski can explain notice confusion, though he may explain three other things first.

speaker: Waldek
text: Mr. Swine's ledger proves usefulness. My order proves why usefulness needs to be seen case by case.

speaker: Waldek
text: If I am assigned to closing argument, I will probably be too plain. Maybe plain is useful.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can say what eviction threatened to take. Let Whimsy say why that matters beautifully.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am ready to stand where the brief needs a home.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: This court is much larger than Housing Court. The fear feels more polished.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I never thought my poster would lead me here. I am not sure it did. Maybe the notice did.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The gallery is full. That helps. Harm should not have to sit alone.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If they speak about public interest, I hope they look at the public seats.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I brought the apartment order folded in my pocket. It is not necessary, but it steadies me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The marble makes everyone quieter. I hope it does not make the facts quieter too.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I can speak if called. My voice is not as steady as I want, but it is mine.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Pig & Swine looks strange in this building. Good. It should be obvious that not every useful door is marble.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the Court asks whether the scheme has individual effects, I can answer before the sentence ends.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am frightened again. But this time I know fear can still speak.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: The notice did not only tell me what might happen. It changed how I lived before anything happened.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: I stopped seeing my apartment as a home and began seeing it as something I had to defend from paper.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: A generic process can feel neutral from above and personal from below.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: In my case, the missing assessment was the whole problem. No one asked whether the response fit the facts.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: The Court stopped the eviction because the rule, the facts, and the consequence had not been properly examined.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: The same danger appears here on a larger scale. The template is doing the work that judgment should do.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: I am not against change. I am against being removed by a process that cannot describe me.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: The individual impact is not an administrative inconvenience. It is the thing administration is supposed to consider.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: If the city had looked at people before printing notices, many of us might not be here.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
context: Supreme Court testimony on individual impact
text: A process that treats different lives identically may be efficient, but it is not necessarily fair.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Public interest should not be a word that makes private consequences disappear.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: When a measure threatens homes, work, and access to legal help, the process must do more than announce itself efficiently.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: In my case, the poster was small and the consequence was enormous. Here, the stated goal is broad and the consequences are still enormous.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: A lawful process should be able to say why this person, this place, this office, this consequence.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the answer is the same for everyone, the question may never have been asked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The Court noticed in my case that pressure had been dressed as rules. This scheme dresses pressure as modernisation.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I do not say the city cannot plan. I say planning should not flatten people into identical recipients.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The harm begins before eviction or closure. It begins when the person realises no one has looked.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: A remedy later is not always enough. Sometimes the process itself pushes people out before they can object.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Proportionality is where the paperwork has to admit there are people underneath.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The district gets to breathe. I know that feeling.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The Court made them restore objections. That means people get back into their own cases.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I am glad my small case was not small in the end.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Pig & Swine stays open. That is good for people who arrive carrying bad weeks.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The notices are not gone forever, but they are no longer unquestioned. That is a beginning.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I will tell the neighbours. Kowalski will pretend he knew the outcome from the acoustics.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Zielińska was right again. Fairness is practical before it is grand.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Thank you for letting me be more than rescued.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: The poster can stay in the window. The judgment can stay in the drawer. Both are useful in different weather.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: If the office ever needs impact statements again, I hope it does not. But I will come.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I brought the poster. Not to hang without permission. Mostly.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: This office still looks like it might collapse. I understand now why people trust it.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Respectable places can make you feel underdressed for your own problem.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Here, no one looked surprised that a small thing mattered.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: I owe Pig & Swine more than thanks. I also owe Asia the return of three labelled folders.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Mr. Pig says the firm is still open. He said it like a weather report from a lifeboat.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Mr. Murrow filed the final judgment as if filing could make relief permanent.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Crab says the paper trail behaved in the end. I think that means congratulations.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Whimsy wants to make the poster the office flag. I have concerns, but fewer than before.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Dr. A. Cula, you helped me keep a home. Then you helped many people keep a door. That is a good use of law.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek kept the apartment, the poster, and a carefully labelled folder of every future notice.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek's poster multiplied twice, then stabilised after Zielińska formed a typography committee.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek now helps neighbours read notices before panic edits them.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek still waters the plant, which has survived eviction, litigation, and Whimsy's praise.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek occasionally visits Pig & Swine with pastries and documents that do not require urgent filing.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek and Kowalski disagree about corridor noise but agree that vague rules are suspicious.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek keeps the court order in the same drawer as the warning letters, because memory is also a safeguard.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek learned that being protected once can become helping someone else speak sooner.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek never became a lawyer, but he became very difficult to intimidate with templates.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.

speaker: Waldek
text: Waldek's window remains visible from Tenants' Row, which is the point.
notes: Voice-reference draft; not committed game text.
